seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kepnock and Keriri Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Kepnock vs Keriri Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kepnock and Keriri Island.
heading: Kepnock vs Keriri Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kepnock, Queensland and Keriri Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kepnock has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Keriri Island (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kepnock or Keriri Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kepnock has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Keriri Island scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kepnock scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Keriri Island scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kepnock or Keriri Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kepnock has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
